name: Pixir upstream customization sync
description: Permission-gated workflow shape for syncing canonical Pixir changes into a customized Pixir target.

patch_id.description: Safe patch identifier already present or planned in patch.md.

canonical_ref.description: Canonical upstream ref, branch, tag, or SHA used as donor head.

target_ref.description: Target ref, branch, or SHA for the customized repository.

approved_scope_note.description: Human-readable summary of the explicitly approved Port/Adapt write scope from patch.md and classification evidence.

steps.task: Check whether patch.md charters {{patch_id}} for syncing {{canonical_ref}} into {{target_ref}} with focus {{focus}}. Name any schema, scope, donor, target, or stop-condition gaps. Recommend the exact uv run scripts/patch_cli.py truth-check command.

Plan classification for {{patch_id}}. Compare the intended canonical ref {{canonical_ref}} against target {{target_ref}} at the level available in this workspace. For each relevant surface, propose Port, Preserve, Adapt, Reject, or Defer and identify the evidence file that should be written under .docs/patches/{{patch_id}}.

Implement only recorded Port and approved Adapt scope for {{patch_id}}. Approved write scope: {{approved_scope_note}}. If the classification requires paths outside this workflow's write_set, stop and request a new concrete workflow with those paths declared. Preserve local Pixir Skills, AGENTS.md law, ADR history, and patch evidence unless the classification explicitly says otherwise. Use deterministic PATCH commands through uv run scripts/patch_cli.py and keep local acceptance separate from CI or external review.

Review the resulting {{patch_id}} handoff. Verify that local acceptance, CI state, external review, and deferred scope are separated. Recommend any missing uv run scripts/patch_cli.py status or accept commands.
